title: Ada and the Magic Library
synopsis: Ada discovers that her school library has magical books that come to life, leading to an adventure with fairy tale characters and a lesson about helping others.

pages.text: Ada loved library time at school. She had curly black hair and bright brown eyes that sparkled when she found good books. Today felt different. The library looked extra sparkly. "Welcome, Ada!" said Mrs. Chen, the librarian. Mrs. Chen had kind eyes behind purple glasses. "I have something special to show you today."

illustration.description: A cozy school library with colorful bookshelves reaching high. Ada, a young girl with curly black hair and bright brown eyes, stands near Mrs. Chen, an Asian librarian with purple glasses and a warm smile. Magical sparkles float in the air around the books. Sunlight streams through large windows.
illustration.alt: Ada and Mrs. Chen in a magical-looking school library

pages.text: Mrs. Chen pointed to a golden bookshelf in the corner. "These books are very special, Ada. But you must promise to be careful." Ada nodded. She always took good care of books. Mrs. Chen handed her a red book with a golden lock. "This one needs your help," she whispered.

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen holds a glowing red book with an ornate golden lock. Ada reaches out with curious eyes wide. The golden bookshelf behind them shimmers with warm light. Other diverse children can be seen reading at colorful tables in the background.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen showing Ada a magical red book with a golden lock

pages.text: As soon as Ada touched the book, it began to glow! The lock clicked open by itself. "Whoa!" Ada said. The pages flipped open and golden light poured out. "Help us, Ada!" came a tiny voice from inside the book. Ada looked closer. She could see a tiny castle on the page!

illustration.description: Ada holds the open book as brilliant golden light streams from its pages. Her expression is amazed and delighted. A tiny fairy tale castle can be seen within the glowing pages. The library around them is bathed in warm magical light, creating dancing shadows on the walls.
illustration.alt: Ada looking amazed as golden light pours from the magical book

pages.text: Suddenly, Ada felt herself getting smaller and smaller. "Wheee!" she laughed as she shrunk down to the size of a mouse. Then WHOOSH! She was sucked right into the book! Ada landed softly on green grass next to the tiny castle. But now the castle was big! "Welcome!" said a voice.

illustration.description: Ada, now tiny, tumbles through swirling golden light and sparkles. Below her is a fairy tale landscape with a grand castle, rolling green hills, and colorful flowers. Her expression is joyful and excited as she falls. The scene has a dreamy, magical quality with rich saturated colors.
illustration.alt: Ada shrinking and falling into the magical book world

pages.text: Ada turned around. A girl with long braided hair and a blue dress smiled at her. "I'm Rapunzel!" she said. "And I'm Jack!" said a boy with freckles, holding some beans. "We need your help, Ada. The mean giant stole all our story endings! Now we don't know how our tales end!"

illustration.description: Rapunzel, a girl with extremely long braided blonde hair and a flowing blue dress, stands next to Jack, a freckled boy in simple brown clothes holding colorful magic beans. Ada looks up at them with determination. The fairy tale setting has a whimsical castle, rainbow flowers, and fluffy clouds in a bright blue sky.
illustration.alt: Ada meeting Rapunzel and Jack in the fairy tale world

pages.text: "That's terrible!" Ada said. "Stories need good endings!" Just then, they heard loud footsteps. STOMP! STOMP! STOMP! "That's the giant!" whispered Jack. "He lives up there." He pointed to a huge beanstalk that reached the clouds. "But how do we get our endings back?" asked Rapunzel. Ada thought hard.

illustration.description: The three children look up at an enormous beanstalk stretching into puffy white clouds. Ada has a thoughtful expression with her finger on her chin. Rapunzel looks worried while Jack points upward. The giant's shadow can be seen in the clouds above, creating an ominous but not too scary presence.
illustration.alt: Ada, Rapunzel, and Jack looking up at the giant's beanstalk

pages.text: "I have an idea!" Ada said. "Maybe the giant is just lonely. Maybe he took the endings because he wants friends to tell stories with!" Rapunzel and Jack looked surprised. "Let's climb up and ask him nicely," Ada suggested. So up the beanstalk they went. Climb, climb, climb!

illustration.description: Ada climbs the thick green beanstalk with Rapunzel and Jack following behind her. Ada's expression is confident and determined. Rapunzel's long hair flows behind her as they climb. Jack grins excitedly. The view below shows the tiny fairy tale kingdom getting smaller as they ascend through soft, colorful clouds.
illustration.alt: Ada, Rapunzel, and Jack climbing the giant beanstalk together

pages.text: At the top, they found the giant sitting alone. He was huge but had sad eyes. "Hello!" called Ada bravely. The giant looked down. "Oh! Visitors!" he boomed. "I'm sorry I took your endings. I just wanted someone to read stories with me. I get so lonely up here." His voice was like gentle thunder.

illustration.description: A gentle giant with kind, sad eyes sits on a cloud. He's enormous but has a friendly face with a bushy beard. Ada, Rapunzel, and Jack stand before him, looking small but brave. The giant's home is cozy with giant-sized books scattered around. The setting is soft and cloud-like with warm, welcoming colors despite the giant's intimidating size.
illustration.alt: The three children meeting the lonely but friendly giant in the clouds

pages.text: "We can be your friends!" Ada said. "And we can tell stories together!" The giant's face lit up with joy. "Really?" he asked. "Really!" said all three children. The giant happily gave back all the story endings. Then they all sat together and told the most wonderful tales. Soon it was time for Ada to go back.

illustration.description: The giant sits cross-legged with a huge smile as Ada, Rapunzel, and Jack sit on his hands like living chairs. Colorful story endings float around them like magical butterflies. Everyone is laughing and happy. The scene is warm and joyful with golden sunset colors painting the clouds around them.
illustration.alt: Everyone happily sharing stories together with the giant

pages.text: "Thank you, Ada!" said Rapunzel and Jack as the golden light surrounded her again. WHOOSH! Ada grew back to her normal size in the library. Mrs. Chen smiled. "Did you help them?" she asked. "Yes!" Ada grinned. "And I made new friends too!" From that day on, Ada knew that sometimes the best way to solve problems is with kindness.

illustration.description: Ada stands in the school library, back to normal size, holding the now-closed red book. Mrs. Chen smiles proudly at her. The golden bookshelf glows softly behind them. Other diverse children are reading happily at nearby tables. The library feels warm and magical, filled with the promise of more adventures.
illustration.alt: Ada back in the library with Mrs. Chen, holding the magical book

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What made the library special for Ada?
choices: It had golden bookshelves with magical books
It had the biggest books in the world
It had no books at all
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen showed Ada a golden bookshelf with special magical books that were different from regular books.

questions.question: Who did Ada meet inside the magical book?
questions.explanation: Ada met Rapunzel, who had long braided hair, and Jack, who had freckles and magic beans.

questions.question: What problem did the fairy tale characters have?
choices: The giant stole their story endings
questions.explanation: The mean giant had stolen all their story endings, so they didn't know how their tales were supposed to end.

questions.question: Why did the giant take the story endings?
choices: He was lonely and wanted friends
questions.explanation: The giant was lonely and took the endings because he wanted someone to read stories with him.

questions.question: How did Ada solve the problem?
questions.explanation: Ada solved the problem by being kind and offering to be the giant's friend so they could share stories together.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete Ada's adventure!
pages.text: Ada found a ____ book in the library. When she opened it, ____ light poured out. She met Rapunzel and Jack who needed help because the giant stole their story ____. Ada solved the problem by offering the giant ____.

pages.title: Character Match
pages.instructions: Match each character with what they had or did!
pages.text: Mrs. Chen wore ____ glasses. Rapunzel had long ____ hair. Jack carried magic ____. The giant felt ____ and wanted friends.
